message.content:
{
  "learning_outcomes": [
    {
      "unit_name": "Physical World and Measurement",
      "chapter_name": "Units and Measurements",
      "topic": "SI Units, Significant Figures, Uncertainty, Dimensional Analysis",
      "outcomes": [
        "Apply fundamental and derived SI units for physical quantities.",
        "Determine appropriate significant figures in measurements.",
        "Calculate and express uncertainty in experimental results.",
        "Perform dimensional analysis to verify equations and derive relationships."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Kinematics",
      "chapter_name": "Motion in a Straight Line",
      "topic": "Linear Motion, Calculus Applications, Graphs, Equations of Motion",
      "outcomes": [
        "Use differentiation and integration concepts to describe linear motion.",
        "Distinguish between average speed/velocity and instantaneous velocity.",
        "Analyze motion using velocity-time and position-time graphs.",
        "Apply graphical and calculus methods for uniformly accelerated motion."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Kinematics",
      "chapter_name": "Motion in a Plane",
      "topic": "Vector Operations, Projectile Motion, Circular Motion",
      "outcomes": [
        "Perform addition, subtraction, and scalar multiplication of vectors.",
        "Resolve vectors into rectangular components using unit vectors.",
        "Calculate scalar and vector products for physical quantities.",
        "Analyze projectile motion and uniform circular motion in a plane."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Laws of Motion",
      "chapter_name": "Laws of Motion",
      "topic": "Newton's Laws, Momentum, Impulse, Friction, Centripetal Force",
      "outcomes": [
        "Explain inertia and apply Newton's first law in various contexts.",
        "Relate force, mass, acceleration via Newton's second law and calculate impulse.",
        "Apply the principle of conservation of linear momentum derived from Newton's third law.",
        "Analyze equilibrium conditions involving static/kinetic friction and centripetal forces."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Work, Energy and Power",
      "chapter_name": "Work, Energy and Power",
      "topic": "Core Concepts of Work, Energy, and Power",
      "outcomes": [
        "Understand the core concepts of Work, Energy and Power.",
        "Apply key ideas from Work, Energy and Power to solve curriculum-aligned problems.",
        "Analyze relationships between major concepts in Work, Energy and Power."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Motion of System of Particles and Rigid Body",
      "chapter_name": "System of Particles and Rotational Motion",
      "topic": "Center of Mass, Torque, Angular Momentum, Equilibrium, Moment of Inertia",
      "outcomes": [
        "Apply conservation of momentum principles to center of mass motion in a system of particles.",
        "Analyze relationships between torque, angular momentum, and their conservation laws.",
        "Evaluate conditions for rigid body equilibrium using equations of rotational motion.",
        "Differentiate linear and rotational kinematic quantities through comparative analysis."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Gravitation",
      "chapter_name": "Gravitation",
      "topic": "Universal Gravitation, Gravity Variation, Potential Energy, Orbital Mechanics",
      "outcomes": [
        "Apply the Universal Law of Gravitation to calculate forces between celestial bodies.",
        "Determine variations in acceleration due to gravity with altitude and depth.",
        "Calculate gravitational potential energy and potential for satellite systems.",
        "Derive escape speed and orbital velocity using conservation of energy principles."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Properties of Bulk Matter",
      "chapter_name": "Mechanical Properties of Solids",
      "topic": "Elastic Behavior, Stress-Strain, Material Constants, Energy Storage",
      "outcomes": [
        "Apply Hooke's Law to determine deformation limits for solid materials.",
        "Calculate Young’s modulus and Bulk modulus from experimental data or problem scenarios.",
        "Analyze the relationship between stress, strain, and elastic potential energy.",
        "Evaluate Poisson's ratio in the context of material deformation."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Properties of Bulk Matter",
      "chapter_name": "Mechanical Properties of Fluids",
      "topic": "Fluid Dynamics, Viscosity, Surface Tension, Bernoulli's Theorem",
      "outcomes": [
        "Apply Stokes' Law to determine terminal velocity of spherical bodies in viscous media.",
        "Distinguish between streamline and turbulent flow regimes using critical velocity concepts.",
        "Utilize Bernoulli’s Theorem to analyze fluid dynamics problems involving pressure, speed, and height.",
        "Calculate surface tension effects including excess pressure across curved surfaces."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Properties of Bulk Matter",
      "chapter_name": "Thermal Properties of Matter",
      "topic": "Heat Transfer, Specific Heats, Latent Heat, Thermal Expansion, Blackbody Radiation",
      "outcomes": [
        "Explain the phenomenon of anomalous expansion in water and its physical significance.",
        "Apply calorimetry principles using specific heat capacities (Cp, Cv) for energy calculations.",
        "Analyze phase transitions involving latent heat during changes of state.",
        "Evaluate thermal conductivity effects across conduction, convection, and radiation modes."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Thermodynamics",
      "chapter_name": "Thermodynamics",
      "topic": "Thermal Equilibrium, State Variables, Thermodynamic Laws, Gaseous Processes",
      "outcomes": [
        "Explain thermal equilibrium and apply Zeroth law concepts to define temperature.",
        "Differentiate between heat, work, and internal energy using the First Law of Thermodynamics.",
        "Analyze reversible/irreversible isothermal and adiabatic gaseous processes."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Behaviour of Perfect Gases and Kinetic Theory of Gases",
      "chapter_name": "Kinetic Theory",
      "topic": "Microscopic Molecular Behavior, Pressure, Temperature, Equipartition Energy",
      "outcomes": [
        "Explain the derivation of pressure and temperature based on kinetic theory assumptions.",
        "Apply the law of equipartition of energy to determine specific heat capacities of gases.",
        "Calculate RMS speed, mean free path, and utilize Avogadro's number in gas problems."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Oscillations and Waves",
      "chapter_name": "Oscillations",
      "topic": "Periodic Motion, Simple Harmonic Motion (S.H.M), Uniform Circular Motion",
      "outcomes": [
        "Distinguish between general periodic motion and Simple Harmonic Motion using time period and frequency.",
        "Formulate displacement as a function of time for S.H.M systems.",
        "Relate the equations of Uniform Circular Motion to the dynamics of oscillations."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Oscillations and Waves",
      "chapter_name": "Waves",
      "topic": "Wave Characteristics, Propagation, Superposition, Interference",
      "outcomes": [
        "Describe the basic properties and classification of mechanical waves.",
        "Analyze the relationship between frequency, wavelength, and wave velocity in different media.",
        "Explain the phenomenon of superposition and interference in wave systems."
      ]
    }
  ]
}
